title: Bistecca alla Fiorentina T Bone Steak

seo.title: How to Make Bistecca alla Fiorentina T Bone Steak — Easy Italian Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Bistecca alla Fiorentina T Bone Steak. This easy Italian recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.

seo.imageAlt: Authentic Italian Bistecca alla Fiorentina T Bone Steak recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Bistecca alla Fiorentina T Bone Steak — a beloved Italian classic

description: Bistecca alla Fiorentina is a magnificent T-bone steak, traditionally from Chianina cattle, cooked rare over hot coals. This iconic Tuscan dish celebrates the pure flavor of high-quality beef, seasoned simply with salt and pepper. It is a true culinary masterpiece, perfect for sharing and savoring the essence of Italian tradition.

ingredients.notes: at least 2 inches thick, Chianina or similar high-quality beef

instructions.title: Bring Steak to Room Temperature
instructions.text: Remove the T-bone steak from the refrigerator at least 1 hour before cooking to allow it to come to room temperature. This ensures even cooking and a better sear. Pat the steak thoroughly dry with paper towels on all sides; moisture is the enemy of a good crust.

instructions.title: Season the Steak Generously
instructions.text: Just before you are ready to cook, season the steak very generously on both sides with coarse sea salt and freshly ground black pepper. Do not be shy with the salt; it is essential for developing that signature crust and enhancing the flavor.

instructions.title: Preheat Grill or Cast Iron Skillet
instructions.text: Heat a charcoal grill to a very high temperature, ensuring the coals are glowing hot. Alternatively, place a large, heavy cast-iron skillet over high heat until it is smoking hot. For an authentic taste, a charcoal grill is preferred, but a cast-iron skillet yields excellent results indoors.

instructions.title: Sear the Steak
instructions.text: Carefully place the seasoned steak directly onto the hottest part of the grill or into the screaming hot skillet. Cook for 5-7 minutes per side for a rare to medium-rare steak, depending on thickness. For a very thick cut, you may also sear the bone side for a few minutes. Aim for an internal temperature of about 125-130°F (52-54°C) for rare.

instructions.title: Rest the Steak
instructions.text: Once the steak is cooked to your desired doneness, remove it from the heat and transfer it to a clean cutting board. Tent it loosely with aluminum foil and let it rest for at least 10-15 minutes. This crucial step allows the juices to redistribute throughout the meat, ensuring a tender and flavorful result.

instructions.title: Slice and Serve
instructions.text: After resting, slice the steak off the bone, then cut it into thick, generous strips. Arrange the slices back around the bone on a serving platter for an impressive presentation. Drizzle with a high-quality extra virgin olive oil and sprinkle with a little more coarse sea salt and fresh rosemary, if desired. Serve immediately and enjoy!

tags: Bistecca alla Fiorentina T Bone Steak
tips: Always bring your steak to room temperature before cooking for even heat distribution and a perfect sear.
Use coarse sea salt generously just before cooking to help create a perfect crust. Do not salt too early, or it will draw out moisture.
Resist the urge to cut into the steak immediately after cooking; resting is crucial for juiciness and flavor. This is non-negotiable for a great steak.
article: Bistecca alla Fiorentina T Bone Steak is a beloved Italian Dinner Recipes dish enjoyed across Italian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
